title: Pull-down on chat header reveals search
design_brief: Pull down on a chat thread reveals a search bar above. Reference: iOS Mail's pull-to-search.
mobile_reasoning: Scroll handler tracks negative offset (overscroll). Search bar height interpolated from offset.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const PTS = {searchH:44, threshold:30};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Inherits ScrollView.
accessibility_considerations: Gesture-only behavior is mirrored by accessibilityActions and a visible label.
performance_notes: Gesture values stay on the UI thread through shared values; React state is not updated per frame.

title: Sheet with horizontal swipe between detail pages
design_brief: Bottom sheet showing item detail; swipe horizontally to next/prev item without dismissing sheet. Reference: Airbnb listing detail.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Dismiss + open new sheet per item
mobile_reasoning: Sheet body uses horizontal PagerView or FlashList horizontal with paging.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Horizontal pan.
accessibility_considerations: Sheet uses accessibilityViewIsModal and includes an explicit close action.
performance_notes: Translation and backdrop opacity are derived on the UI thread with one shared value.
what_makes_this_native: Uses native platform primitives rather than web-only assumptions, so touch, keyboard, safe areas, and accessibility stay coherent.

title: FlashList with section sticky header sliding under
design_brief: Section headers slide under each other as user scrolls. Reference: Apple Music library, Contacts.
mobile_reasoning: FlashList stickyHeaderIndices handles the slide-under natively.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
solution: // Already covered in rn-024 via stickyHeaderIndices prop\nimport {FlashList} from '@shopify/flash-list';\nexport function StickyList<T>(props: any) { return <FlashList {...props} stickyHeaderIndices={props.headerIndices}/>; }
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: FlashList uses stable keys, memoized rows, and estimated sizes to keep scrolling predictable.
what_makes_this_native: Uses native platform primitives rather than web-only assumptions, so touch, keyboard, safe areas, and accessibility stay coherent.
